title: Implement the transactional outbox
description: Add an outbox table, write events within the business transaction, and relay them to the broker at-least-once.
body: Steps: 1. Create an outbox table (id, aggregate, type, payload, created_at, published_at). 2. In each command's transaction, insert the outbox row alongside the state change. 3. Build a relay (polling worker or CDC like Debezium) that reads unpublished rows in order, publishes to the broker, and marks published. 4. Make the relay idempotent so a crash between publish and mark re-publishes safely (consumers dedup). 5. Add cleanup/retention for published rows. Rollback point: disable the relay; events queue safely in the table. Validation: a committed write yields one published event, a rolled-back write yields none, confirmed by tests.
